nodes.name: Weekly Trigger
nodes.notes: Fires every Monday 07:00 UTC. All 5 branches run in parallel from here.

documentId.cachedResultName: AI Customer Feedback Synthesis & Product Roadmap Prioritiser

nodes.name: Fetch NPS (Google Sheets)
nodes.notes:
Tab: nps_responses. Columns: timestamp, score, comment, email, plan, source, week_tag.
Populate via Google Form (see SETUP section) or manual paste.

documentId.cachedResultName: AI Customer Feedback Synthesis & Product Roadmap Prioritiser

nodes.name: Fetch Support Issues (Google Sheets)
nodes.notes:
REPLACES FRESHDESK.
Tab: support_issues. Columns: timestamp, issue_title, description, severity (1-5), reported_by, plan.
Users/team members fill a Google Form (Support Intake Form) — auto-appends to this tab.
Alternatively paste copy-pasted support emails here manually each week.

documentId.cachedResultName: AI Customer Feedback Synthesis & Product Roadmap Prioritiser

nodes.name: Fetch Call Notes (Google Sheets)
nodes.notes:
REPLACES GMAIL SALES CALL NOTES.
Tab: call_notes. Columns: date, contact_name, plan_type, key_points, pain_mentioned.
After each sales/customer call, paste a brief summary into this tab.
No email labels, no Gmail API — just a plain sheet. Works for any team size.

documentId.cachedResultName: AI Customer Feedback Synthesis & Product Roadmap Prioritiser

nodes.name: Fetch Community Posts (Google Sheets)
nodes.notes:
Tab: community. Columns: date, platform, content, author, upvotes.
Paste in Discord/Slack/Twitter posts manually, or link via Zapier free tier.

nodes.name: IF: Has Feedback?
nodes.notes: TRUE → continue to AI. FALSE → notify Slack and stop cleanly.

parameters.text:
=ℹ️ *Feedback Digest — {{ $json.meta.week }}*

No feedback was collected this week across all 5 sources. Nothing sent to AI.

_Sources checked: NPS sheet, Support sheet, Reddit mentions, Call notes, Community sheet._

documentId.cachedResultName: AI Customer Feedback Synthesis & Product Roadmap Prioritiser

options.systemMessage:
=You are a senior product analyst specialising in customer feedback synthesis.

You will receive a JSON array of feedback items from multiple sources:
- nps (survey scores and comments)
- support (support tickets and issues)
- reviews (Reddit mentions and public posts)
- sales (notes from customer calls)
- community (Discord/Slack community posts)

Each item has:
- source: nps | support | reviews | sales | community
- text: the raw feedback text
- score: numeric score if available (null if not)
- user_plan: customer plan tier (unknown if not available)

YOUR TASK:

1. Read ALL feedback items carefully.

2. Cluster them into 8–12 thematic groups based on the underlying product need or pain point.

3. For each theme compute:
- frequency: count of items that belong to this theme
- sentiment_score: float from -1.0 (very negative) to 1.0 (very positive)
  - Use score where available
  - Infer from text otherwise
- impact_score: float from 0–10
  - Increase impact when:
    (a) mentioned by paid/pro users
    (b) appears across 2+ sources
    (c) mentions churn, revenue loss, or switching tools

4. Suggest ONE concrete, specific feature or fix per theme.

5. Rank themes using:
priority_score = (0.4 * freq_ratio) + (0.35 * impact/10) + (0.25 * urgency)

Where:
- freq_ratio = frequency / total_items
- urgency = (1 - sentiment_score) / 2

6. Assign priority_rank from 1 (highest priority) to N.

7. Write a 3–5 sentence executive summary highlighting the most critical signals.

RULES:

- Use only the provided input. Do NOT invent data.
- Frequency cannot exceed total input items.
- Theme names must be 3–6 words.
- Prioritise cross-source signals (same issue across multiple channels).
- Do NOT over-prioritise high-volume but low-impact noise.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Return ONLY a valid JSON object (no markdown, no explanation):

{
  "themes": [
    {
      "theme": "string",
      "frequency": integer,
      "sentiment_score": float,
      "impact_score": float,
      "priority_rank": integer,
      "suggested_feature": "string",
      "sample_quotes": ["string"],
      "sources": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "summary": "string"
}
